questions.question: What is the key difference between a page fault and a segmentation fault? (3 marks)
keyPoints: A page fault occurs for a valid virtual address that is not currently in physical memory.
It is handled by loading the required page from disk.
A segmentation fault occurs for an invalid virtual address (outside the process's allocated space) and typically results in process termination.

questions.question: Describe in detail the steps the operating system takes from the moment a page fault trap occurs until the user process is restarted. (10 marks)
keyPoints: Trap to kernel mode and save process state.
Check if memory access is legal (within process's virtual address space). If not, terminate process.
Determine the disk location (backing store) of the required page.
Find a free frame in physical memory (if none free, run page replacement algorithm).
If page replacement is needed, check the victim page's dirty bit. If set, schedule a write to disk.
Schedule a disk read operation to load the required page into the free frame.
Block the current process and context switch to another process while waiting for I/O.
Upon disk I/O completion interrupt, receive control and update page table (set valid bit, frame number).
Move the process that faulted back to the ready queue.
When scheduled, restart the instruction that caused the page fault.
